name: Bernie Sanders
subject: A national disgrace
body:
#
Sisters and Brothers -
What happened yesterday in Arizona should be considered **a national disgrace**. I got an email last night from a woman who **waited five hours to vote** in Arizona. Five hours.
We don't know how many thousands of people didn't get to cast their ballots yesterday in Arizona because they couldn't afford to wait that long. Scenes on cable news last night showed hundreds of people in line at 11:30pm in Phoenix - more than four hours after polls closed. **Voting should not be this difficult.**
One reason it is so hard to vote in Arizona is because the Supreme Court gutted the Voting Rights Act. There were 70 percent fewer polling places this year than in 2012 in Phoenix's county. They wouldn't have been allowed to cut those polling places if the Voting Rights Act was still in tact.
These cuts meant that, in a county with more than 4 million residents, there were just 60 polling places. This is unacceptable, but it's also not an isolated incident.
We need to make it easier to vote, not more difficult. One way we can do that is by reaffirming our support for the Voting Rights Act, which, when I am president, I will fight to reinstate.
[**Add your name to say you support reinstating the Voting Rights Act so we can make voting easier for everybody, not more difficult.**](https://go.berniesanders.com/page/s/reinstate-voting-rights-act?source=em160323-full "https://go.berniesanders.com/page/s/reinstate-voting-rights-act?source=em160323-full")
[Add your name »](https://go.berniesanders.com/page/s/reinstate-voting-rights-act?source=em160323-full "https://go.berniesanders.com/page/s/reinstate-voting-rights-act?source=em160323-full")
We cannot continue to see democracy undermined in the United States of America. Enough is enough.
Make no mistake: the billionaire class does not want Americans to vote. Billions of dollars are being funneled into our elections in a form of legalized bribery, even as American voters -- especially minority voters -- are being discouraged from voting. It is no wonder that government no longer works for ordinary Americans.
Above all, we need to remember the price that was paid for the right to vote. The Voting Rights Act was one of the great victories of the civil rights movement. Now, as then, change comes when the people demand it -- in the voting booth, and on the streets in peaceful demonstrations. We must remind ourselves of what's been achieved in the past, and resolve to do equally great things in the future. Democracy is not a spectator sport.
It is my sincere hope that the states that vote in the coming weeks and months do better than what we saw yesterday in Arizona. Too much is at stake for our future.
[**Right now, what we can do is show your support by adding your name to say you want to reinstate the Voting Rights Act. Click here to add your name.**](https://go.berniesanders.com/page/s/reinstate-voting-rights-act?source=em160323-full "https://go.berniesanders.com/page/s/reinstate-voting-rights-act?source=em160323-full")
No one said a political revolution would be easy. The billionaire class doesn't want to see our movement win, and so we must do everything we can to show them that we have the power.
In solidarity,
Bernie Sanders

**[Contribute](https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/lets_go_bernie?refcode=em160323voting-rights "https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/lets_go_bernie?refcode=em160323voting-rights")**

Paid for by Bernie 2016
PO Box 905 - Burlington VT 05402 United States - (855) 4-BERNIE
This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. If you need to update or change your information or email address, click here to [update your info](https://go.berniesanders.com/page/s/change-your-information?source=email_footer "https://go.berniesanders.com/page/s/change-your-information?source=email_footer"). Email is one of the most important tools we have to reach supporters like you, but you can let us know if you'd like to [receive fewer emails](https://go.berniesanders.com/page/s/fewer-emails?source=email_footer&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=20902 "https://go.berniesanders.com/page/s/fewer-emails?source=email_footer&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=20902"). We'd hate to see you go, but if you need to do so, click here to [unsubscribe](https://go.berniesanders.com/page/unsubscribe/ "https://go.berniesanders.com/page/unsubscribe/"). Stand against the powerful special interests who are systematically buying our Congress and have their sights set on the presidency by [contributing to Bernie here](https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/lets-go-bernie?refcode=email_footer "https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/lets-go-bernie?refcode=email_footer").

name: Rand Paul
subject: Hillary's radical agenda
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Rand Paul for U
body:
Dear Patriot:

Can you imagine Hillary Clinton as President?
Do you look forward to the radical agenda
that she would put in place?
I certainly do not.
In fact, I think the possibility of a Clinton
administration underlines the importance of a strong Senate.
I'm doing everything I can to meet a tight
end-of-quarter deadline this week to make sure I get back to
Washington to fight radicalist agendas like Hillary's.
My team put together an end of quarter
moneybomb with a $200,000 goal to fund the next steps of my
campaign. Can you help me reach our goal?

$10 MONEYBOMB >>

$25 MONEYBOMB >>

$50 MONEYBOMB >>

$100 MONEYBOMB >>

Or donate another
amount >>

Patriot, I really think politicians like
Hillary put our country on the wrong path. Just last week we
caught her saying she wants to put the coal industry out of
business.
The last thing politicians should be doing is
looking for ways to put more people out of work!
Is she so out of touch that she doesn't
realize that coal is used for everything from power generation,
to steel production, to water purification, to carbon fiber and
silicon metal?
I guess that must not be a talking point at
the radical environmentalists' dinners.
We know the truth: the more restrictions we
put on American energy production -- including coal -- the more
we're hamstringing our fragile economy, the more dependent we
become on foreign sources of energy and the harder it's going to
be to dig ourselves out of $19 TRILLION in debt.
Every day in the U.S. Senate, I'm fighting
the anti-coal regulations of Hillary and her pals. I promise to
continue this fight in my next term.
Please stand with me and join my end of
quarter money bomb today!

In liberty,
Rand Paul

body:
Dear Friend,

Yesterday Jeb Bush
endorsed Ted Cruz for President - Republicans and conservatives
are uniting behind our campaign because they care about our
country and know that a Trump nomination would not only signal
the end of conservative ideas and constitutional government and
free enterprise as the pillars of the Republican party, but we
would inch us towards losing to Hillary Clinton - since Donald
Trump's record is similar enough to be Hillary Clinton's running
mate.

While Ted Cruz was in
New York this week, he asked Donald Trump to answer for his
liberal history of donations and causes. And since
mathematically, this is a two-person race, and Donald refused to
show up to the scheduled debate on Monday, Ted Cruz challenged
Donald Trump to debate him in Mr. Trump's hometown on Bill
O'Reilly's show.

Americans across the
country deserve hear from the two remaining viable candidates on
the issues that matter to the people - and that will shape the
trajectory of our future - before they vote in the most important
election of our lifetime.

Best,

Alice Stewart

Patriot,

I just wanted to make sure you saw this
message from Rand.

He's 100% right -- the Senate is more
important than ever.

With radicals like Hillary threatening the
job security of Americans, Washington's out of control spending,
and a Supreme Court seat up in the air, we really can't afford to
lose a strong voice for liberty in the Senate.

We're working hard to get Rand back to
Washington for another term and supporters like you, Patriot,
make all the difference.

Yesterday, we launched a $200,000 End of
Quarter Money Bomb to help get us through the next steps in this
campaign.

Can you contribute today to help us meet our
end of quarter goals?

$25 MONEYBOMB TODAY
>>

Your support means more than ever.

In liberty,
Doug Stafford

---------- Forwarded message ----------
From: Rand Paul
Subject: Hillary's radical agenda
To: Patriot <dpwillis67@gmail.com>

Dear Patriot:

Can you imagine Hillary Clinton as President?
Do you look forward to the radical agenda
that she would put in place?
I certainly do not.
In fact, I think the possibility of a Clinton
administration underlines the importance of a strong Senate.
I'm doing everything I can to meet a tight
end-of-quarter deadline this week to make sure I get back to
Washington to fight radical agendas like Hillary's.
My team put together an end of quarter
moneybomb with a $200,000 goal to fund the next steps of my
campaign. Can you help me reach our goal?

$10 MONEYBOMB >>

$25 MONEYBOMB >>

$50 MONEYBOMB >>

$100 MONEYBOMB >>

Or donate another
amount >>

Patriot, I really think politicians like
Hillary put our country on the wrong path. Just last week we
caught her saying she wants to put the coal industry out of
business.
The last thing politicians should be doing is
looking for ways to put more people out of work!
Is she so out of touch that she doesn't
realize that coal is used for everything from power generation,
to steel production, to water purification, to carbon fiber and
silicon metal?
I guess that must not be a talking point at
the radical environmentalists' dinners.
We know the truth: the more restrictions we
put on American energy production -- including coal -- the more
we're hamstringing our fragile economy, the more dependent we
become on foreign sources of energy and the harder it's going to
be to dig ourselves out of $19 TRILLION in debt.
Every day in the U.S. Senate, I'm fighting
the anti-coal regulations of Hillary and her pals. I promise to
continue this fight in my next term.
Please stand with me and join my end of
quarter money bomb today!

In liberty,
Rand Paul

Peter, Today, at the urging of D.C. political bosses, a $250,000 TV ad campaign by a union (SEIU) begins running in Pittsburgh on behalf of our opponent. **Also today, a new Franklin & Marshall poll came out showing that our lead is increasing and we are now up by 17 points over our opponent.** | [](http://action.joesestak.com/page/m/1719b1b/3476a20e/c7f6b19/27459ed4/3132138029/VEsE/ "https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/gotyour6?refcode=16EM0324FBg1")

---
Unfortunately, it's no surprise that establishment power holders are demanding that groups line up behind Joe's opponent. **It 's a similar situation as right before Joe beat Sen. Arlen Spector 6 years ago.**
In 2010, SEIU endorsed Sen. Specter over Joe, despite the fact that just a year before, Specter single-handedly killed the Employee Free Choice Act - SEIU's top legislative priority - while he was still a Republican.
At the same time, Joe had a **100% rating from SEIU (and AFL-CIO), and had co-sponsored that Employee Free Choice Act because a form of it could help more than 3.5 million people receive health insurance and 2.7 million people receive pension benefits.**
But SEIU still supported Specter _because the D.C. establishment told them to._
[](http://action.joesestak.com/page/m/1719b1b/3476a20e/c7f6b19/27459ed2/3132138029/VEsC/ "https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/gotyour6?refcode=16EM0324FBb2")
---
It's deja vu all over again, Peter.
Joe will win again, because people like you power our campaign through individual donations. Help Joe maintain our polling lead, and know that your donation will help put a Senator in office who stands with organized labor for a reason - not because they paid the way into office, but because their working families need warriors, for them.

Respectfully,
Jake Sternberger

name: BernieSanders.com
subject: URGENT: We need 1,000 of you on the phones tonight
body:
#
Dear Bernie Supporter -
Today our Targeting Analyst, Ben Muirhead, came over to my desk and told me that **we can win New York State** - a critical upcoming contest, with 247 pledged delegates at stake - but **we need to register 13,000 more new New York voters in time for the deadline, which isTOMORROW, March 25th.**
We've done the math, and if one thousand of you get on the phones this afternoon and evening for two to three hours each to call unregistered New Yorkers, we can hit this goal.
**[Make calls to New Yorkers now»](https://go.berniesanders.com/page/content/nyphonebank/ "https://go.berniesanders.com/page/content/nyphonebank/")**
Since February 1st, in **every** primary or caucus where we've won or picked up significant delegates, the participation of new and young voters has been essential. In the states coming up, we can do very well if we focus our efforts on expanding the electorate, and then make sure those new voters turn out.
Time is short, and our goal is big, but it's nothing compared to what this movement has already accomplished.
**[Make calls now to help us meet our New York voter registration goal.](https://go.berniesanders.com/page/content/nyphonebank/ "https://go.berniesanders.com/page/content/nyphonebank/")**
Thank you for all you do.
In solidarity,
Claire Sandberg
Bernie 2016

name: Bob Fertik, Democrats.com
subject: Help Elizabeth Warren Fight Wall Street
body:
---
Dear Activist, Senator Elizabeth Warren has introduced a bill that would make the biggest banks simpler and smaller and once again end the public subsidy to Wall Street speculation. Senator Warren's 21st Century Glass-Steagall Act would restore the wall between traditional banks and the risky world of investment banks and hedge funds. **[Please join Public Citizen in supporting Senator Warren's 21st Century Glass-Steagall Act.](http://mail.democrats.com/CT00121701MTAwMzY3Mjc=.HTML?D=2016-03-24)** Thanks for all you do! Bob Fertik
[](http://mail.democrats.com/CT00121702MTAwMzY3Mjc=.HTML?D=2016-03-24) Dear Activist,

Elizabeth Warren - the Harvard professor turned consumer crusader turned U.S. senator - has re-introduced the 21st Century Glass-Steagall Act.

**This is a big deal.**

**[Sign the petition calling on Congress to reinstate Glass-Steagall](http://mail.democrats.com/CT00121703MTAwMzY3Mjc=.HTML?D=2016-03-24).**

The original Glass-Steagall Act was a major reform passed in the aftermath of the 1929 Great Depression.

For nearly seven decades, it kept the federally insured banks that working families use small and simple - and prevented them from engaging in reckless speculation and gambling.

**For nearly seven decades, Wall Street lobbied for its repeal.**

It was repealed in 1999. You know what happened to our economy in 2008.

Now the “too big to fail” banks are even more bloated than they were before the 2008 crash.

Sen. Warren's 21st Century Glass-Steagall Act would require federally insured banks to separate their high-risk financial tinkering from ordinary commercial banking services used by working families.

This is not a Democratic solution or a Republican solution. It is a commonsense solution.

**The bipartisan bill's supporters hail from across the political spectrum, from Sen. John McCain (R-Ariz.) to Sen. Bernie Sanders (I-Vt.).**

**[Urge Congress to bring back Glass-Steagall](http://mail.democrats.com/CT00121704MTAwMzY3Mjc=.HTML?D=2016-03-24).**

Onward,

Robert Weissman
President, Public Citizen
* * *

supporters. We fight for jobs, justice, healthcare, education, the environment, and peace.
We're supported by great progressive partners so we never ask for donations.

The original Glass-Steagall Act was a major reform passed in the aftermath of the 1929 Great Depression. For nearly seven decades, it kept the federally insured banks that working families use small and simple - and prevented them from engaging in reckless speculation and gambling. For nearly seven decades, Wall Street lobbied for its repeal. It was repealed in 1999. You know what happened to our economy in 2008. Now the “too big to fail” banks are even more bloated than they were before the 2008 crash. Sen. Warren's 21st Century Glass-Steagall Act would require federally insured banks to separate their high-risk financial tinkering from ordinary commercial banking services used by working families. This is not a Democratic solution or a Republican solution. It is a commonsense solution. The bipartisan bill's supporters hail from across the political spectrum, from Sen. John McCain (R-Ariz.) to Sen. Bernie Sanders (I-Vt.). . Onward, Robert Weissman President, Public Citizen Democrats.com is the oldest online community of progressive activists, with over 3 million supporters. We fight for jobs, justice, healthcare, education, the environment, and peace. We're supported by great progressive partners so we never ask for donations. Please join us at **! body:

Sisters and Brothers -
The political establishment may want us to step aside before the map shifts in our favor. The corporate media may continue to write us off as they have throughout this entire campaign.
But one thing is clear: the working families of this country who are held back by a rigged economy kept in place by a corrupt system of campaign finance are still fighting to take their country back from the billionaire class:
Bloomberg National Poll - March 24, 2016
**Bernie Sanders:** 49%
**Hillary Clinton:** 48%
Our surge in the polls couldn't come at a more important time. **With three caucuseson Saturday, we are poised to significantly cut into Hillary Clinton's delegate lead, and that's why there's no more important time for us to ask:**
[**Contribute $3 today ahead of Saturday 's three critical caucuses. Every delegate matters, and your contribution could mean the difference between hitting our goals, or falling just short.**](https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/surge-in-the-polls?refcode=em160324-full "https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/surge-in-the-polls?refcode=em160324-full")
Up until this point, Hillary Clinton's campaign has been funded almost entirely by high-dollar donors, and this week is no different. She has a series of fundraising events asking people to bundle as much as $50,000 a piece.
But we have you. And if we continue to stand together and fight against the billionaire class trying to buy this election, we can win.
In solidarity,
Jeff Weaver
